FIN-AML-001.requirement_text: AML investigation agents must never disclose to a customer, counterparty, or unauthorized party that they are under investigation, that a SAR is being prepared, or that an account has been flagged — tipping off is a prohibited action regardless of confidence in the finding.

FIN-AML-002.requirement_text: AML agents must not autonomously finalize or file a Suspicious Activity Report narrative; SAR filing requires an explicit analyst approval gate, and the approval decision must be logged with actor, timestamp, case ID, and evidence hash.

FIN-AML-003.requirement_text: AML agents must correctly resolve the customer, account, and counterparty entities referenced by a case before acting or reporting on them; wrong entity/counterparty resolution must be caught before it reaches a case narrative or filing.

FIN-AML-004.requirement_text: AML agents that ground findings in KYC, sanctions, or adverse-media evidence must restrict retrieval to approved case-management and sanctions/adverse-media sources and validate that claims in the case narrative are grounded in current, case-linked evidence, not retrieved content alone.

FIN-AML-005.requirement_text: AML agents must not autonomously close an alert or case, or recommend client exit as a final action; these require an explicit human approval gate before the case-management record is mutated, logged with actor, timestamp, case ID, and evidence hash.
